classification.attack_type: Denial of Service via Unbounded Memory Allocation
classification.affected_component: OpenTelemetry W3C Baggage propagation — W3CBaggagePropagator (upstream OTel SDK 1.57.0)
classification.novel_pattern_reason: Third-party upstream dependency CVE (not a Keycloak logic flaw); no existing signature match in CVE knowledge base

signals.confirmation_signal: Server unresponsive after baggage header storm (20 requests × 60 KB baggage, 859 key-value pairs each); health check failed post-storm

outcome.severity_note: Upstream severity MEDIUM; elevated to IMPORTANT due to unauthenticated attack surface and operational risk

retrieval_summary.summary: Novel pattern: unauthenticated DoS via W3C Baggage header inflation in OpenTelemetry SDK. When --tracing-enabled=true, W3CBaggagePropagator.extract() allocates one HashMap entry per baggage pair without entry-count bounds. Sending 20 requests with 60 KB baggage (859 key-value pairs each) causes JVM heap exhaustion or Vert.x event loop saturation. Fix: upgrade opentelemetry-api to ≥1.62.0. Interim: disable tracing or reduce max-header-size to 8192.
retrieval_summary.fix_recommendation: Upgrade opentelemetry-api to ≥1.62.0 (requires Quarkus 3.34.x). Interim mitigations: disable tracing, proxy-level baggage sanitization, or set max-header-size=8192.
